articles.title: TENNESSEE PUTS INSTAGRAM'S ADDICTIVE DESIGN ON TRIAL AS META FACES A SEVEN-WEEK JURY FIGHT
articles.summary: Meta went on trial in Nashville to face Tennessee's claim that it deliberately designed Instagram features to keep teenagers compulsively engaged. The seven-week jury trial, split into liability and penalty phases, could impose damages up to $1,000 per violation and force design changes. Meta denies wrongdoing and has cited Section 230 protections. The case joins a broader wave of state litigation, including a $1.4 trillion claim from four states heading to trial in California this August.

articles.title: WHATSAPP'S LIQUID GLASS REDESIGN IS FINALLY COMING TO MAC AFTER MONTHS OF WAITING
articles.summary: WhatsApp is rolling out a Liquid Glass redesign for its Mac app, bringing it in line with the iPhone and iPad versions through refreshed navigation, updated menus, and dedicated sections for Locked Chats and Communities. The update focuses on visual consistency rather than new features, with the redesign gradually reaching more Mac users over the coming weeks.

articles.title: VIBECODED APPS ARE FLOODING APPLE'S APP STORE, AND USERS ARE GETTING SICK OF THEM
articles.summary: App submissions to Apple's App Store have surged due to AI-assisted "vibecoding," nearly doubling in early 2026 compared to last year's already elevated numbers. Downloads have grown only marginally by comparison, and reviewers, developers, and forum communities report increasing frustration with low-effort, derivative, or unstable apps flooding the platform. Communities like r/iosapps have introduced moderation systems requiring vibecoded apps to be flagged, reflecting growing skepticism even toward legitimate new releases.

articles.title: NINE DESIGNERS OPEN UP ABOUT AI-ASSISTED CODING
articles.summary: Nine designers, ranging from major tech companies to early-stage startups, shared candid views on AI-assisted coding and its effect on their craft. Opinions split between those energized by finally shipping real products themselves and those worried it pulls focus away from deeper problem-solving and user research. The consensus: AI coding tools bring real value when used with discipline, but can create friction with engineers and erode quality if leaned on carelessly.

articles.title: IS THE TINDER REBRAND REALLY A SUCCESS? NOT EVERYONE IS SO SURE…
articles.summary: Tinder's first rebrand in a decade introduces a bolder visual identity and a fictional dating columnist, "T," to position the platform around authenticity and real connections for Gen Z. While the branding has been widely praised, critics argue its message clashes with upcoming AI-powered features that raise privacy concerns, suggesting the user experience will ultimately determine whether the rebrand succeeds.

articles.title: HALFTONE, DITHER, & ASCII EDITOR
articles.summary: Bitgrain is a browser-based editor for making posters, album covers, zines, and music videos with deterministic effects — halftone, dither, ASCII, pop art, and more.

articles.title: FREE TEXT MOCKUP GENERATOR
articles.summary: Type anything and instantly see it as an iMessage, WhatsApp, Instagram, TikTok, Notes, or Google mockup.

articles.title: WHICH AI?
articles.summary: Which AI? is a daily guessing game. Spot whether each landing page was designed by Gemini, Claude, or GPT. New challenge every day.

articles.title: SCULPTING A SHAPELESS MEDIUM
articles.summary: Intent, a new tool for coding with AI agents, organizes work into workspaces rather than single agent chats, aiming to keep structure in an otherwise shapeless medium. Rather than one general chatbot, the tool uses specs, specialist agents, and bundled workspaces so people can steer AI work instead of just prompting it. Since launch, users have shown mixed reactions — some confused by the new orchestration model, others finding it supercharges their workflow, while rising token costs from parallel tasks have emerged as an unexpected tradeoff.

articles.title: WHERE DOES DESIGN RESIDE NOW?
articles.summary: Design work has fragmented across three destinations: template-driven tools like Canva, individual employees using generative AI, and constraint systems governing model outputs, dissolving the traditional design department. Two divergent futures compete—design roles shrinking into obsolescence as prompts replace brand books, or elevating designers into systems architects who govern AI-generated output quality. DesignOps is emerging to address resulting brand-consistency chaos, mirroring Digital Asset Management's 2010 fragmented-market trajectory, while smaller agencies lose entry-level work permanently to internalized, AI-assisted production.

articles.title: KOTO HELPS RESTAURANT-DISCOVERY APP FRANKI MAKE TIME DANCE
articles.summary: Koto rebranded restaurant discovery app Franki around the idea of "Make Time Dance," creating a playful, community-focused identity that encourages people to discover, share, and earn rewards from real dining experiences. The new branding combines warm typography, vibrant colours, expressive motion, and a character-like logo to help Franki stand out in the crowded restaurant recommendation market while reinforcing its shift from a discovery tool to a social platform.

articles.title: ADOBE STOCK AI STUDIO VS. CANVA AI VS. CREATIVE FABRICA: WHICH AI EDITOR ACTUALLY WINS?
articles.summary: Testing across three weeks found no single overall winner.

articles.title: 14 LOGOS THAT DEFINED AMERICAN GRAPHIC DESIGN HISTORY
articles.summary: Wallpaper*'s Anatomy of a Logo series profiles 14 iconic American logos, tracing how design ingenuity and marketing created enduring emblems across fashion, food, sport, art and music.
